metadata.title: The Chromatic Manipulation: How Color Psychology Controls Emotion in Music

metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Infants categorize colors by six months and can name them by eighteen months. Color meaning is cultural: green and white carry opposite connotations across regions. Editors control feeling by ratioing calm scenes against brief high-stimulation ones. That scene duration ratio runs roughly four-to-one up to twelve-to-one.

artifactLines: Infants categorize colors by six months and can name them by eighteen months.
Color meaning is cultural: green and white carry opposite connotations across regions.
Editors control feeling by ratioing calm scenes against brief high-stimulation ones.
That scene duration ratio runs roughly four-to-one up to twelve-to-one.

beats.narration_text: The developmental timeline, categorizing colors by six months and naming them by eighteen, plus the scene duration ratio of 4:1 to 12:1, made color feel like a controllable dial. Walk me through: (1) why color categorization precedes naming in infant development, (2) how green or white can flip meaning between Western and Eastern contexts, and (3) how I would use that low-to-high stimulation scene ratio to build tension in a short music video.

beats.narration_text: By six months, they can categorize colors. By eighteen months, they can name them.

beats.narration_text: Green represents nature and growth in Western symbolism, but in certain East Asian contexts it can symbolize infidelity. White signifies purity and simplicity in Western traditions, but in many Eastern cultures it is associated with mourning and death.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Green represents nature and growth in Western symbolism, but in certain East Asian contexts it can symbolize infidelity. White signifies purity and simplicity i

beats.narration_text: Scene Duration Ratio = (Low-Stimulation Scene Length 7–12 s) ÷ (High-Stimulation Scene Length 1–3 s) ≈ 4:1 to 12:1
